titleStmt.title: Letter from Ellen Daly to Kate Allman, 28 March 1923
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Ellen Daly

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Mother of Charlie Daly, Ellen Daly is mourning Charlie's death. She supports their family friend, Kate Allman's decision to return to the convent to become a nun. 1 page

msIdentifier.collection: P41 Charlie Daly Collection, Location MG2 B6 S4, Fond 10: "Correspondence of Kate Allman", Sub-Fond 3 "Correspondence between Kate Allman and the Charlie Daly's Siblings"

correspAction.placeName: Convent of Mercy, Waterford, Co. Waterford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
841/10/3/3/2 Thursday Dear Katty Oh how hard it is to write when
ones heart is sore. God holy will be done
Oh Katty what a thunderbolt when we thought
our darling was safe he was too good for
this mean world & is gone to a happier home
well prepared to meet his ? ? but left
sad hearts in this miserable world my
darling had no secrets from me even to
the last. I will treasure them during my
life. Well Katty I am delighted you have
left the outside world. If you ever come
home if, God, spares my life I will go to see you
your nice letters were very consoling.
Katty excuse me I can't write any more
my heart is too full.
God Bless ? ? you is the
? of your ever loving friend
Ellen ? letter
